items.title: What is the 70 rule in deer hunting?

expanded_element.title: Shed Hunting: Unveiling the 70% Rule - Tactacam
expanded_element.description: The 70% rule simplifies shed hunting timing. The idea is to wait until approximately 70% of the bucks have shed their antlers before hitting the woods. This timing minimizes the risk of pushing deer around and maximizes your chances of finding antlers.

expanded_element.title: Exploring the Meaning of "Hunting Lodge" - YouTube
expanded_element.description: The term comprises two words hunting which is the activity of chasing. And killing wild animals. And Lodge a type of house or shelter.

items.title: What are those hunting huts called?

expanded_element.title: Hunting blind - Wikipedia
expanded_element.description: A hunting blind (US), hide or machan is a concealment device or shelter for hunters or gamekeepers designed to reduce the chance of detection by animals. There are different types of blinds for different situations, such as deer blinds and duck blinds.

items.title: What is the 7 day rule for deer?

expanded_element.title: Unlocking the 7-Day Rule: How to Pattern and Tag Mature Bucks Year ...
expanded_element.description: The 7-Day Rule is a strategy built around the fact that mature bucks often revisit specific scrapes, trails, or stand sites during the same 7-day period every year, sometimes down to the exact day.

items.title: What is the 7 day rule for deer hunting?

expanded_element.title: Best Time to Hunt Whitetail Deer (2025) | HuntWise
expanded_element.description: A: The “7-day rule” suggests deer often return to a particular area about seven days after a major weather or pressure change, like a cold front. Use a hunting app (like HuntWise) to track the weather and the whitetail deer hunting forecast based on movement in your area.

items.title: Where do big bucks usually hide?

expanded_element.title: How To Find A Big Buck's Core Area - Deer Hunters' Moon Guide
expanded_element.description: Besides the obvious areas of thick cover, bucks will retreat to city limits, industrial parks, wildlife sanctuaries or even tracts of land that are off limits to hunters completely … anywhere they can hide without being seen or bothered once hunting pressure begins.
